data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-23T12:42 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.thinking:
Deacon, checking in. I need to follow the startup protocol exactly:

1. Announce: "Deacon, checking in." (ONE line)
2. Then IMMEDIATELY run: `bd show skogtown-wisp-wisp-0sysw`
3. Begin execution

Wait, the hooked work says `skogtown-wisp-wisp-0sysw` but the patrol status section shows `skogtown-wisp-wisp-15qi9t` as the current patrol ID. Let me follow the instructions exactly - the hook says to show `skogtown-wisp-wisp-0sysw`.


Looking at the hook section more carefully, the bead `skogtown-wisp-wisp-0sysw` has all its children marked as completed, which means this patrol is finished. I should run the heartbeat check first, then verify the hook status, and finally display the hooked bead as instructed.

content.content: ✓ Heartbeat updated

? skogtown-wisp-wisp-0sysw [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic
Created: 2026-02-22 · Updated: 2026-02-22

DESCRIPTION
Deacon patrol cycle.

Monitors Gas Town agents (Mayor, Witnesses), processes lifecycle requests,
and handles escalations. Created as wisps (ephemeral, not synced to git).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-3ujyw: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-78tsv: Scan agent health ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-c7tku: Check for orphaned processes ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-ogvg8: Decide: loop or hand off ● P2
